data.title: U.S. Budget Deficit Sets a December Record as Tariff Revenue Cools
data.description:
In December, the U.S. budget deficit rose by 67% year-over-year to $145 billion, setting a record high for the month but below the consensus estimate of $152.5 billion. In addition, December's tariff revenue was $27.9 billion, the lowest amount since July and down from $30.75 billion in November and $31.35 billion in October.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, the U.S. Treasury noted that after accounting for adjustments, the December budget deficit would have fallen by 11% from a year ago to $112 billion. One of the adjustments accounted for the transfer of $32 billion of January benefit payments into December because the new year began on a weekend.

U.S. on Track for $2 Trillion Budget Deficit in FY2026

While tariff revenue has fallen in recent months, total customs duties for fiscal year 2026, which began on October 1, 2025, have increased by 333% from the comparable period last year as a result of the Trump administration's tariffs.

Since the beginning of the fiscal year, the budget deficit has totaled $602 billion, down by 15% compared to the same period a year ago. The U.S. is on track to reach a deficit of nearly $2 trillion this fiscal year, according to Maya MacGuineas, president of the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget. The deficit was $1.7 trillion in the 2025 calendar year, marking a three-year low.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Government Clears Nvidia (NVDA) to Export H200 Microchips to China
data.description:
The government of U.S. President Donald Trump has eased restrictions placed on the export of Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 microchips to China.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Multiple media reports say that the Trump implementing a rule that will likely enable shipments of these advanced AI chips to begin, according to the federal register.

Before any H200 chips can be exported to China, the U.S. Commerce Department requires that shipments undergo review by a third-party testing laboratory to confirm their technical AI capabilities.

Additionally, Nvidia must certify that there are sufficient H200 chips available in the United States before any exports to China can proceed.

This regulatory adjustment marks a shift in U.S. policy regarding the export of advanced semiconductor technology to China, though with specific verification requirements in place.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Nvidia Denies Upfront Payments, Making H200 Chips Easier to Buy
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has reportedly said that it does not require upfront payments for its H200 chips. According to Reuters, a company spokesperson said Nvidia would never ask customers to pay for products they have not received. The statement came after an earlier Reuters report claimed Nvidia had imposed strict upfront payment terms on Chinese buyers of its AI chips.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The H200 chip is part of Nvidia's growing range of advanced hardware that companies are eager to buy as they race to build and train AI systems, despite tighter U.S. export rules and closer scrutiny from Chinese regulators.

Nvidia Lowers Barriers to H200 Chips

Nvidia is making it easier for customers to buy its H200 chips by removing upfront payment requirements. This move reduces financial risk for buyers and could help boost demand, especially amid uncertainty over regulatory approvals in China. By lowering these barriers, Nvidia positions the H200 for broader adoption while maintaining its leadership in AI chip technology.

In the past, Nvidia's standard terms for Chinese customers often required advance payments, though some buyers were allowed to make a deposit instead of paying in full upfront. For the H200 chip, however, Nvidia has been stricter because of uncertainty over whether Chinese regulators would approve the shipments, the source said.

Under such payment terms, much of the financial risk would shift from Nvidia to its customers, who would have to commit funds without knowing whether Beijing would approve the imports or whether they could use the chips as planned.

Nvidia Sees Strong H200 Demand

Despite regulatory uncertainty, demand for Nvidia's H200 chip remains strong. Reuters previously reported that Nvidia already has orders for more than two million H200 chips, priced at around $27,000 each, representing roughly $54 billion in potential revenue. At CES, CEO Jensen Huang stated that demand for the China-focused H200 is "very strong" and that he does not anticipate major regulatory issues.

For many Chinese companies, the H200 is currently the most advanced chip they are legally allowed to buy. However, Chinese regulators have asked some tech firms to temporarily pause orders while they review requirements to purchase more domestically made chips alongside Nvidia's products.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Sell Stock?

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $264.97, Nvidia's average share price target implies a 43.3% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Pull Back as Core Inflation Cools; Tariff Revenue Shrinks
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Tuesday's trading session in negative territory, shrugging off an encouraging inflation reading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In December, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 2.7% year-over-year, in line with the consensus estimate of 2.7% and unchanged from November. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 2.6% from a year ago, below the estimate of 2.7% and holding steady from the prior month.

Despite easing prices in recent months, inflation remains above the Fed's target of 2%. Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem noted that the inflation data is "encouraging," although he still sees "little reason" to cut interest rates in the near term. He believes that rates are currently around a neutral level, which means that they neither stimulate nor restrict economic growth.

Investors seem to agree, with the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings all drifting lower in response to the inflation data. The Fed is widely expected to hold rates steady during its first FOMC meeting of the year on January 28, with CME's FedWatch tool pricing in 97.2% odds.

"JUST OUT: Great (LOW!) Inflation numbers for the USA," President Trump said in a Truth Social post on Tuesday. "That means that Jerome 'Too Late' Powell should cut interest rates, MEANINGFULLY!!!"

Trump also promised to support protesters in Iran amid an uprising over economic challenges and political repression. On Monday, he ordered a 25% tariff on all countries that trade with Iran, which could potentially fray the U.S.-China trade truce. China purchased over 80% of Iran's oil exports in 2025 and is its largest trading partner. Other key partners include Russia, India, and Japan.

Elsewhere, the U.S. budget deficit notched a monthly record high of $145 billion in December as tariff revenues fell to $27.9 billion, registering the lowest amount since July. However, it's worth noting that after adjustments, the budget would have fallen to $112 billion, marking a year-over-year decrease of 11%, or $14 billion. A major adjustment was accounting for the transfer of $32 billion in January benefit payments into December because the new year began on a weekend. Additionally, while tariff revenue has cooled in recent months, it's still up by 333% from the comparable period last year since the start of fiscal year 2026 on October 1, 2025.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.19% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.18%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Netflix Stock (NFLX) Jumps on Reports It Will Make All-Cash Offer for Warner Bros. Discovery
data.description:
Shares of streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) are up 1% in after hours trading on reports the company is planning to revise its takeover bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) to make it an all-cash offer.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

WBD stock is up 2% on the reports that Netflix is planning to sweeten its offer for the entertainment giant. Bloomberg was first to report that Netflix is looking to make its $72 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. an all-cash bid.

News of the revised proposal comes with Netflix embroiled in a takeover battle for Warner Bros. Discovery against Paramount Skydance (PSKY). The board of directors at Warner Bros. has endorsed Netflix's takeover offer and repeatedly rejected Paramount Skydance's bid as "inferior." However, Paramount is not going away.

The Battle for Warner Bros.

Most recently, Paramount Skydance made a hostile bid of $30 per share for all of Warner Bros. Discovery, including its legacy television networks that include CNN, HBO, and the Food Network. After the board at Warner Bros. rejected that offer, Paramount launched a lawsuit.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Offers Free Starlink Internet in Iran amid Deadly Protests
data.description:
Elon Musk's aerospace firm SpaceX is now providing free Starlink internet service in Iran amid deadly protests and a nationwide internet blackout. Ahmad Ahmadian, executive director of the U.S.-based group Holistic Resilience, said that SpaceX has removed the monthly subscription fee so that people who already have Starlink receivers in Iran can connect without paying. A person familiar with Starlink's operations also confirmed this with Bloomberg, although SpaceX itself did not comment publicly.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This move shows how Starlink has become an important tool in global politics during times of crisis. In fact, Musk has used Starlink in similar situations before, including in Ukraine, where it has provided internet for civilians and the military. Starlink also offered free broadband in Venezuela earlier this year after political upheaval from U.S. action against President Nicolás Maduro.

Meanwhile, unrest in Iran has intensified, with hundreds of thousands of people protesting against Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. To make matters worse, activist groups are warning that thousands may have died over more than two weeks of violence. However, even though Starlink receivers are illegal in Iran, Ahmadian estimates that more than 50,000 units have been smuggled into the country. Unfortunately, Iranian authorities are now trying to jam Starlink signals and track users, according to Amir Rashidi of the Miaan Group.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $401.93 per share implies 10.1% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Alibaba Stock Spiked and What Jefferies Is Watching Now
data.description:
Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) shares started the week on a strong note, rising 10.5% after reports showed that the company's Qwen AI models had surpassed 700 million downloads on Hugging Face. In fact, December downloads alone exceeded the combined total of the next eight most popular models on the platform.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move aligns with the broader bullish stance of Jefferies analyst Thomas Chong, who recently named Alibaba a "top pick in 2026," citing opportunities across AI, cloud computing, and its integrated consumption platform.

Operationally, Chong expects Alibaba to start extracting meaningful synergies from its Quick Commerce business, with losses narrowing sequentially and cloud revenue growth beginning to reaccelerate. At the same time, the analyst flags that losses in the "All Others" segment are likely to widen, reflecting heavier investment in AI-driven initiatives such as the Qwen app, DingTalk, AI glasses, and other projects, as well as continued spending on Amap.

Still, Chong's optimism on the longer-term opportunity doesn't translate into near-term exuberance. The analyst remains more cautious than the broader Street on several December-quarter metrics. For the quarter, he forecasts total revenue of RMB287 billion, implying year-over-year growth of 2.5%, below the consensus estimate of 4.5% and his own prior forecast of 4%.

Within the China E-commerce Group, Chong models revenue of RMB162 billion, versus the Street at RMB168 billion and his earlier estimate of RMB166 billion. The analyst also sees customer management revenue growth of just 2% y/y, compared with consensus expectations of 6.3% and his previous 6.5% call. The shortfall, in his view, reflects base effects tied to service fees and QZT, as well as a broader slowdown in industry GMV growth.

Data from the National Bureau of Statistics of China underscores that cooling trend. Industry GMV growth ran at ~7.5% y/y in the September quarter, before easing to 4.9% in October and just 1.5% in November.

Separately, the analyst has kept his estimate for Cloud Intelligent Group revenue growth at 35% y/y. For Alibaba International Digital Commerce Group (AIDC), the losses should narrow to around RMB2 billion (compared with a RMB5 billion loss in December 2024) vs. a prior estimate of breakeven. Overall, Chong projects EBITA of roughly RMB27.5 billion, below the Street at RMB36.8 billion and his prior estimate of RMB38.9 billion, with an EBITA margin of about 9.6%, vs. consensus at 12.6% and his previous call of 13.3%. These figures account for QC losses of RMB22 billion, in line with expectations, and All Others losses of RMB8.5 billion, compared with the consensus forecast of RMB4.2 billion and Chong's prior estimate of RMB3.8 billion.

All told, Chong assigns BABA shares a Buy rating, and his $225 price target makes room for 12-month returns of 35%. (To watch Chong's track record, click here)

13 other analysts join Chong in the bull camp, while 1 Hold obviously can't detract from a Strong Buy consensus rating. The forecast calls for 12-month returns of 22%, considering the average price target clocks in at $203.75. (See BABA stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: "The Number Two Chip Foundry," Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Blasts Up on New Appraisal
data.description:
Could chip stock Intel (INTC) eventually become bigger than TSMC (TSM)? Maybe, but that is not really the point. Could Intel advance to the point where it is second only to TSMC? That assessment is a lot more likely, and it recently came from KeyBanc Capital Markets. The analysis works wonderfully in Intel's favor, and Intel investors roared approval. Intel shares jumped nearly 8% in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst John Vinh, who has a five-star rating on TipRanks, offered up upgrades for both Intel's rating and its price target. Vinh upgraded Intel from Sector Weight to Overweight, and also upgraded the price target from its original $40 per share to $60.

In fact, Vinh advanced the notion that Intel could ultimately surpass Samsung (SSNLF), the current second-largest chipmaker around, based on the progress Intel has shown so far with the 18A and 14A nodes. Vinh actually pointed out that the 18A node alone could be enough to "…convince us it could credibly be the No. 2 foundry supplier in the industry ahead of Samsung." Yields on 18A have improved to over 60%, and that is more than enough to beat Samsung's SF2 process. Throw in the 14A node waiting in the wings and things only look better for Intel.

Rising Tides Lifting Boats

Intel's recent expansion efforts, and its scaling back on unnecessary expenses, have left it in a great position overall as the demand for chips is only likely to increase from here. With Intel becoming an increasingly large part of the picture for AI data servers—and AI in general on something of a rise—the end result is potentially a huge win for Intel.

While a lot of work on AI is done with graphics processing units (GPUs), a point where Intel has historically lagged, the more traditional central processing unit (CPU) is starting to come back into vogue to work with the GPU for better results. Intel's bread and butter is CPUs, and with Intel now nearly "sold out for the year" on data center CPUs, it may need to raise prices on any further sales. That, again, is a win for Intel.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on seven Buys, 17 Holds and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 129.48% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $40.96 per share implies 13.39% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Fed's Musalem Sees 'Little Reason' for Rate Cuts
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem believes that current interest rates are positioned appropriately around a neutral level, while inflation will ease toward the Fed's target of 2% this year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I see little reason for near-term further easing of policy," he said, adding that inflation data released this morning is "encouraging." In December, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) and core CPI rose by 2.7% and 2.6%, respectively, on an annual basis.

Musalem Sees Lower Inflation Risks, Emphasizes Fed Independence

Musalem added that the risk of higher inflation has eased, with current rates supporting the Fed's dual mandate without over-stimulating or restraining economic growth.

Furthermore, Musalem stressed that the Fed should remain independent, as it leads to stable inflation and higher employment. "Central bank independence with respect to monetary policy is a valuable asset for a country," he said.

data.title: Merck Sees $70B Revenue Potential From New Growth Drivers by Mid-2030s
data.description:
Pharma giant Merck (MRK) is targeting $70 billion in revenue from new growth drivers by the mid-2030s, as the company accelerates the launch of new drugs to mitigate the impact of the loss of business from the patent expiration of its blockbuster cancer drug Keytruda in 2028. This new target marks a step up from the company's previously announced estimate of $50 billion in new growth opportunities. Merck discussed its new target and growth drivers at the 44th annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference held on Monday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Merck Seeks to Boost Business via Organic Growth and Strategic Deals

During the event, Merck's CEO Robert Davis expressed confidence that the company would achieve $50 billion of its $70 billion target by the early 2030s. Davis added that the company's optimism is based on 10 major programs that make up 70% of the $70 billion estimate. Merck views all these programs as multibillion-dollar opportunities. In fact, four of the 10 programs are either already launched or have positive Phase III data, with the remaining expected to have important Phase III readouts over the next 12 to 18 months. 

According to Reuters, Merck expects cardiometabolic and respiratory treatments to contribute nearly $20 billion in sales, up from its previous ⁠forecast of $15 ​billion. Also, infectious disease drugs are estimated to generate about $15 billion, compared with the earlier projection of $5 billion.

Additionally, the company is open to further strategic acquisitions. According to Bloomberg, CEO Davis said, "We are not limited from a balance sheet — it's more where do we see strategic opportunity."

The CEO's comments come amid reports that Merck is in talks to buy cancer drugmaker Revolution Medicines (RVMD). Citing sources, the Financial Times reported last week that Merck is considering acquiring Revolution Medicines for $28 billion to $32 billion.   

Is Merck Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Merck stock based on 11 Buys and five Holds. The average MRK stock price target of $119.53 indicates 9.5% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
